properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.enabled
properties.description:
Enable/disable the MCP server.

When set to false, the MCP server and all its components will not be initialized.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.instructions
properties.description:
The instructions of the MCP server instance.

These instructions are used to provide guidance to the client on how to interact with this server.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.name
properties.description:
The name of the MCP server instance.

This name is used to identify the server in logs and monitoring.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.prompt-change-notification
properties.description:
Enable/disable notifications for prompt changes. Only relevant for MCP servers with prompt capabilities.

When enabled, the server will notify clients when prompt templates are modified.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.request-timeout
properties.description: Sets the duration to wait for server responses before timing out requests. This timeout applies to all requests made through the client, including tool calls, resource access, and prompt operations.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.resource-change-notification
properties.description:
Enable/disable notifications for resource changes. Only relevant for MCP servers with resource capabilities.

When enabled, the server will notify clients when resources are added, updated, or removed.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.sse-message-endpoint
properties.description:
The endpoint path for Server-Sent Events (SSE) when using web transports.

This property is only used when transport is set to WEBMVC or WEBFLUX.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.stdio
properties.description:
Enable/disable the standard input/output (stdio) transport.

When enabled, the server will listen for incoming messages on the standard input and write responses to the standard output.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.tool-change-notification
properties.description:
Enable/disable notifications for tool changes. Only relevant for MCP servers with tool capabilities.

When enabled, the server will notify clients when tools are registered or unregistered.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.tool-response-mime-type
properties.description: (Optional) response MIME type per tool name.

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.type
properties.description:
The type of server to use for MCP server communication.

Supported types are:

  • SYNC - Standard synchronous server (default)
  • ASYNC - Asynchronous server

properties.name: spring.ai.mcp.server.version
properties.description: The version of the MCP server instance.
